linguisticChallenges.polysynthesis: Cree is highly polysynthetic. A single verb can incorporate subject/object pronouns, instrumentals, locations, and actions. Translate phrases conceptually rather than word-for-word.
linguisticChallenges.animacy: Verb conjugation changes completely based on whether the subject/object nouns are animate or inanimate. Ensure grammatical agreement.
linguisticChallenges.neologisms: Avoid literal translations of modern software concepts. Maintain English loanwords for modern UI elements when no community-accepted Cree term exists.

encyclopedic.history: Plains Cree is the most widely spoken Algonquian language in Canada. Historically, it functioned as a regional trade lingua franca across the Canadian plains.
